tone_directive: Handheld 16mm intimacy in Baltic winter — white sky pressing down, dark water below, sodium orange splitting the night. Clinical distance punctuated by single moments of unbearable proximity. Sound recorded on-location: industrial hum underneath everything, even indoors.
style_seed: cinematic photography, desaturated palette, shallow depth of field, Baltic winter light, film grain, 35mm, natural lighting

assets.prompt: A woman, 42, short dark hair graying sharply at the temples — not dyed, not managed. Jaw set. Eyes making contact a beat too long before they move away. Dark wool coat, collar turned up. Standing on the metal-grate platform of a harbor crane, one hand on the railing, knuckles loose but the grip is there. Below her: container stacks, fog, sodium floodlights. White sky above. She is not looking at the camera — she is looking at something the camera cannot see. cinematic photography, desaturated palette, shallow depth of field, Baltic winter light, film grain, 35mm, natural lighting
assets.negative_prompt: smiling, glamorous, soft focus, bright colors, warm light, illustration, stock photo, AI skin, dramatic pose, looking at camera with intention

assets.dialogue: The manifest was filed at 06:12. The crane log says 06:40. That's twenty-eight minutes nobody wants to account for. I'm not asking you to explain it. I'm telling you I noticed.

voice_params.tone: flat, precise, zero emotional register — the absence of affect is the threat
voice_params.pacing: deliberate, short sentences with hard stops, silence before the key phrase
voice_params.accent: Northern German, educated, flattened — the regional edge sanded off but not gone

assets.prompt: Solo cello, low register, 58 BPM, single repeated four-note motif with a half-step descent on the third note. No vibrato. Dry acoustic — recorded in a concrete room with no reverb treatment. After sixteen bars, a second cello enters a minor third below, playing the same motif slightly behind — never synchronizing. No resolution. The two voices never align. 30 seconds total.

A woman in a dark coat climbs the exterior ladder of a harbor crane in the pre-dawn dark. She reaches the operator platform and stops. She puts one hand on the steel railing — not gripping it, just touching it. Below: container terminal, floodlights cutting through fog, cranes motionless. She does not look down. She looks at the horizon where the water goes gray before sunrise. Wind moves her hair slightly. She stands there. 8 seconds, handheld camera from below looking up, desaturated, film grain.

A man, 38, tall, runner's frame carrying desk weight in the shoulders. Wearing a pressed dark suit — the jacket fits well, the setting does not. Standing in a fluorescent-lit customs corridor, glass partition offices behind him, paper stacks visible through the glass. He holds a coffee cup in his left hand. His right hand is at his side, thumb touching the wedding ring without rotating it yet. Eyes slightly unfocused — he is calculating, not seeing. Three-day stubble on a face that used to be careful about it. cinematic photography, desaturated palette, shallow depth of field, Baltic winter light, film grain, 35mm, natural lighting
assets.negative_prompt: smiling, action pose, bright colors, warm light, heroic framing, illustration, stock photo, AI skin, neat appearance

assets.dialogue: I've logged every contact, every shipment authorization, every variance in the inspection schedule going back fourteen months. The pattern is there. It's in the pattern. I just need the access to show it to someone who doesn't already know.

voice_params.tone: measured, slightly too careful — a man choosing each word like it will be read back to him later
voice_params.pacing: even tempo with occasional micro-pauses mid-sentence, as if self-correcting in real time
voice_params.accent: Hamburg German, formal register, the politeness that comes from being an outsider trying to prove himself

assets.prompt: Solo piano, upright acoustic, slightly out of tune on the upper register. 66 BPM. A repeating eight-bar phrase that builds toward a resolution in bar seven and then sidesteps it — not a false ending, a wrong turn. The phrase restarts. Second pass: the same phrase but with one additional note added in bar three that shouldn't be there. Third pass: the additional note arrives earlier. The resolution never comes. Sparse, dry, room ambience only, no processing. 30 seconds total.

A man in a suit runs the perimeter road along the harbor fence at 6am. Gray pre-dawn, sodium lights still on, water visible through the chain-link fence. He runs at a steady pace — not racing, not enjoying it. His breath steams. He passes a gate. Slows slightly. Looks through the fence toward the container terminal. Keeps running. Behind him, the lights of the customs building are already on. 7 seconds, static camera from the side, waist level, desaturated, film grain.

Container terminal at first light, four massive gantry cranes in silhouette against a white Baltic sky, orange sodium floodlights still burning against the coming day, wet concrete apron reflecting the light in broken pools, a single shipping container being lowered into position, the operator cab forty meters up, diesel exhaust rising. Deep focus on the cranes, middle distance blurred slightly. No people visible at ground level. cinematic photography, desaturated palette, shallow depth of field, Baltic winter light, film grain, 35mm, natural lighting
assets.negative_prompt: sunny, daytime, blue sky, people in foreground, bright colors, clean, modern

assets.prompt: Harbor container terminal ambient soundscape, 60 seconds. Layers: constant low diesel engine rumble at 80Hz as foundation, distant hydraulic hiss of crane mechanisms cycling, metal-on-metal clang every 8-12 seconds as containers are set down — never predictable, always heavy, chain rattling and settling. Underneath: faint wind through steel girder frames. Further back: a foghorn across the water, twice in 60 seconds. No music, no processing, recorded acoustic space with significant low-end room resonance.

Interior of a 1970s brutalist federal building, long corridor stretching to a vanishing point, exposed concrete ceiling with fluorescent tube strips running its length, one tube flickering at the far end. On both sides: glass-walled offices with paper files stacked to shoulder height, a coffee machine with a red power light on a counter visible through glass. The corridor is empty. Rain-light through a single window at the end. cinematic photography, desaturated palette, shallow depth of field, Baltic winter light, film grain, 35mm, natural lighting
assets.negative_prompt: modern, glass tower, warm, cozy, color, people, natural light, open plan

assets.prompt: Federal office building ambient soundscape, 60 seconds. Layers: fluorescent light hum at a constant 60Hz, coffee machine heating element click and idle beneath it, distant paper shuffling every 10-15 seconds, a phone ringing twice and stopping unanswered at the 25-second mark. Acoustic quality: linoleum floors, concrete walls, drop ceilings — sound deadened but not absorbed, a mid-range flatness. Occasional distant footstep on hard floor. No music, no exterior sound, sealed building atmosphere.

Narrow harbor-town street at 10pm, old two-story buildings pressing close, cobblestones wet from sea wind. The bar Zur Möwe: a hanging sign swinging, amber light through frosted glass windows, cigarette smoke drifting under the lamp post outside. Two men in work jackets standing by the door, not talking — watching the street. At the far end of the alley, harbor crane tops visible above the roofline. No tourists. cinematic photography, desaturated palette, shallow depth of field, Baltic winter light, film grain, 35mm, natural lighting
assets.negative_prompt: daytime, sunny, modern, tourists, clean, bright, wide streets, neon signage

assets.prompt: Harbor neighborhood bar exterior and street at night, 60 seconds. Layers: distant sea wind moving through a narrow alley, glass bottles and low conversation audible through a bar wall — not words, just the frequency of speech and the clink of glasses. Occasional burst of laughter from inside, door opening and closing once releasing 3 seconds of louder interior sound then cutting back to muffled. A ship horn far out on the water. Cobblestones: a single pair of heavy boots walking away. Cold air acoustic — sound travels flat and far.

Main theme, 30 seconds. Two instruments only: solo cello and solo upright piano, detuned on upper octave. The cello states a descending four-note phrase — Control. The piano answers with an ascending pattern that doesn't resolve — Trust. Neither phrase completes. They overlap at the midpoint, creating a dissonance that neither instrument acknowledges. The cello phrase repeats, lower. The piano phrase repeats, higher. They diverge without conflict. No drums, no strings section, no production treatment. Recorded in an empty concrete room. 58 BPM, ending on an unresolved suspension.

Wide establishing shot of Rostock commercial harbor at dusk from water level, massive container terminal cranes dominating the skyline in silhouette, sodium floodlights activating as daylight fails, the water between camera and docks reflecting orange light in broken streaks, fog beginning to collect around crane bases, a cargo vessel moving slowly left to right in the middle distance. In the foreground: the railing of a dock or smaller vessel, out of focus. No people. The cranes fill the frame like standing figures. cinematic photography, desaturated palette, shallow depth of field, Baltic winter light, film grain, 35mm, natural lighting
assets.negative_prompt: daytime, blue sky, tourists, pleasure boats, bright colors, summer, clean water, modern architecture
